set.body:
Problem

COO.entries allocates a boxed Seq[COOEntry], while CSR primitive storage is intentionally private. COOBuilder construction and add/canonicalization failures throw, so persistence and ingestion boundaries cannot validate and stream triplets through a total Gale API. Scalafim currently retains an allocation-heavy SparseTriplets adapter for its spatial cache.

Scope

Add storage-safe streaming sparse traversal and checked construction:
- COO.foreachStoredEntry and CSR.foreachStoredEntry with a primitive-style (row, col, value) callback and no boxed COOEntry intermediate.
- A checked COO builder factory and checked mutation path, such as tryAdd(row, col, value): Either[LinAlgError, Unit].
- Total checked finalization for invalid shapes, indices, and DuplicatePolicy.Error failures; define clear names for throwing convenience methods versus Either-returning ingestion methods.
- Checked canonical CSR creation honoring the existing DuplicatePolicy and producing canonical row-major CSR output.
- An explicit non-finite value policy. Either values remain allowed by the default contract or validation is selected through a named policy; no silent filtering, coercion, or behavior change.

Acceptance

- COO and CSR traversal visits each stored entry exactly once in deterministic storage order, preserves explicit zeros and non-finite values according to the declared policy, and allocates no boxed entry sequence.
- Checked shape, index, duplicate, and finalization failures return Left(LinAlgError) rather than throwing.
- DuplicatePolicy.Sum, Last, and Error have explicit checked-path tests, and checked CSR output is canonical under the existing Gale definition.
- Throwing legacy builder APIs remain source-compatible unless an intentional breaking change is documented.
- Public traversal does not expose or permit adoption of COO or CSR backing arrays.
- Shared JVM and Scala.js tests cover empty and zero-sized shapes, invalid shapes and indices, duplicate coordinates, all duplicate policies, explicit zeros, NaN and infinities under the named policy, traversal order, and round-trip reconstruction.
- Scalafim persistence can validate, serialize, and reconstruct triplets without calling COO.entries or retaining a compatibility triplet collection.
set.title: Streaming sparse-entry traversal and checked triplet construction
